query: US-Iran conflict status July 2026: is there a ceasefire in effect? Latest on Islamabad MoU, tit-for-tat strikes, Trump statements, negotiations after July 11 2026
answer:
Research complete. Here is the summary of findings:

## US-Iran Conflict Status — July 13, 2026

**No ceasefire is in effect.** The interim ceasefire and the June 17 Islamabad Memorandum of Understanding have collapsed. Trump declared the MoU "over" on July 8, and the two countries are now in an active, escalating exchange of strikes.

**Key findings:**

- **Ceasefire/MoU status:** Dead. Trump declared the MoU "over" July 8 after Iran struck ships in the Strait of Hormuz and the US struck Iranian territory. Reporting describes the June 17 MoU as "effectively defunct."

- **The Islamabad MoU:** A 14-point framework brokered mainly by Pakistan (Qatar, Saudi, Turkey, Egypt facilitating), signed remotely June 17 by Trump (at Versailles after the G7) and President Pezeshkian (Tehran). It provided a 60-day ceasefire extension, toll-free Strait of Hormuz reopening, end of the US naval blockade, and a 60-day oil-sanctions waiver — but deferred all nuclear, missile, and proxy issues.

- **Tit-for-tat strikes:** US conducted a second consecutive night of strikes July 12–13 (dozens of targets, expanding beyond coastal areas). Iran's IRGC declared the Strait of Hormuz "closed until further notice" (July 12) and struck US bases across Bahrain, Kuwait, Qatar, Jordan, and Oman; Jordan intercepted four Iranian missiles July 13.

- **Trump statements:** Called Iran "sick people"/"scum"; threatened "1000 Missiles are Locked and Loaded"; claimed July 10 that Iran "asked us to continue talks" — which **Iran's foreign ministry publicly denied**.

- **Negotiations after July 11:** Indirect diplomacy via Qatar/Oman/Pakistan mediators continues but is strained; the two governments contradict each other on whether talks were even requested. No formal senior-level round has convened.

- **Market signals:** ~58% for a second 2026 ceasefire; ~31% for US-Iran being at war on Jan 1, 2027.

Full synthesis, sources, and quantitative data points have been delivered via the structured output. Note that strike counts and casualty figures are contested and drawn from each side's live-reported claims.

key_facts: As of July 13, 2026, NO US-Iran ceasefire is in effect; the June 17 Islamabad MoU has collapsed and Trump declared it 'over' on July 8, 2026.
The US conducted a second consecutive night of strikes July 12-13, 2026, hitting 'dozens' of Iranian military targets and expanding beyond coastal areas (Bandar Abbas, Qeshm Island).
Iran's IRGC declared the Strait of Hormuz 'closed until further notice' on July 12, 2026, citing US interference; CENTCOM insisted the waterway 'remained open to all vessels.'
The Islamabad Memorandum was a 14-point framework brokered mainly by Pakistan, signed remotely by Trump (at Versailles after G7) and President Pezeshkian (Tehran) on June 17, 2026.

The MoU provided for an end to strikes, toll-free Strait of Hormuz reopening for 60 days, ending the US naval blockade, a 60-day ceasefire extension, and a 60-day oil-sanctions waiver (effective June 21).
The MoU deferred all nuclear/uranium-stockpile, ballistic-missile, and Iranian proxy-network issues to future talks.

Tit-for-tat: Iran struck US/allied bases in Bahrain, Kuwait, Qatar, Jordan, and Oman; Jordan intercepted four Iranian missiles July 13; US strikes cumulatively cited at 90 to 300+ targets across operations.
Trump claimed on July 10 that Iran 'asked us to continue talks'; Iran's foreign ministry denied requesting new negotiations, saying it only accepted a Qatari mediator visit.
Trump called the Iranian regime 'sick people'/'scum' and threatened '1000 Missiles are Locked and Loaded,' vowing to 'completely decimate and destroy all areas of Iran.'
Mediators (Qatar, Oman, Pakistan) continued shuttle diplomacy; VP Vance, envoy Witkoff, and Kushner reportedly engaged in phone diplomacy.
Shipping through the Strait of Hormuz fell sharply amid the crisis (from ~49 vessels July 7 toward ~25-34).
Prediction markets (July 13): ~58% for a second 2026 ceasefire; ~31% US-Iran at war on Jan 1, 2027; ~34% formal end-of-war agreement by Aug 31, 2026.
